title: fix(core): preserve reasoning items across multi-step tool calls for Azure OpenAI

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(@mastra/core): resolve OpenAI reasoning model memory issue
fix(core): store empty reasoning for OpenAI item_reference

summary: Fixes Azure OpenAI rejecting multi-step tool call requests because reasoning items were being silently dropped after the first step. Two root causes: (1) CacheKeyGenerator.fromAIV4Part only checked `providerMetadata.openai.itemId` for dedup keys, so Azure's `providerMetadata.azure.itemId` was ignored and all empty reasoning items got the same key `reasoning0`, causing pushNewPart() to dedup them away. Fix: iterate all provider keys to find itemId. (2) AIV5Adapter dropped empty reasoning parts (no text, no details) even when they carried providerMetadata needed by the API. Fix: preserve parts that have providerMetadata. Includes 192-line test utility and integration into both loop and message-list test suites.
maintainerNotes: High-quality bug fix from a repeat contributor (roaminro also authored #11492 for a related reasoning model issue). The PR is well-documented with clear before/after code snippets, has a changeset, and adds thorough tests. CI shows 'Core Package Tests' and 'Full Test Suite' failing, and 'Tool Builder Tests' pending — these failures need investigation to determine if they are related to this PR or are pre-existing flaky tests. Review is requested from DanielSLew. CodeRabbit flagged one potential issue: `fromAIV5Parts` in CacheKeyGenerator may also need the same provider-agnostic itemId fix applied to `fromAIV4Part` — worth verifying during review.
